text.title: ai-governance-compliance Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-governance-compliance as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running SHAP/LIME explainability on black-box LLM API outputs (GPT-4, Claude) and treating feature attribution as meaningful.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SHAP/LIME feature attribution is designed for tabular ML and tree ensembles. For transformer architectures and black-box APIs, use behavioral testing (perturbation analysis) instead. Agents generating compliance reports using LLMs can hallucinate passing scores — always validate generated reports against ground-truth metrics computed from actual data.

failure-mode.description: Agent that auto-approves a model for production based on compliance metrics, or automated rollback triggered by drift alerts without human review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Automated green lights are insufficient for high-risk AI Act categories. Drift alerts should not trigger automatic rollback without human review — automated rollback can introduce compliance violations if the prior version had known issues. Human-in-the-loop checkpoint is mandatory.
